steps.title: Spot the surprising contrast
steps.content: The second sentence begins with Yet, signaling a contrast. It claims that Ross's parents “would have been correct” if they'd made a prediction about his career. This tells us the first sentence must describe something rare or unexpected.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
”Anticipate” means to predict or expect, and the sentence reads: “Few new parents anticipate that their baby will someday become an astronaut.” This is logical—most parents don't expect their child will have this specific ambitious future.

The other three either describe different actions or fail to express prediction.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
